title: Hypotheses, Riskiest Assumptions, and Disposable Prototypes · Decision Evidence Lens: Prototype Test
summary: A team starts production architecture while desirability, usability, feasibility, viability, safety, and operating assumptions remain untested. Predict the decision, affected people, evidence strength, failure cost, and transfer limit before seeing a worked case. Planned artifact: Add and verify the pp1 prototype test increment in ranked assumption map and prototype evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Run a protocol with success, failure, observation, accessibility accommodation, and interpretation criteria set before the session.
Choose paper, clickable, coded, data, integration, or operational prototype fidelity to answer one question without production overinvestment.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# personal-project-1 cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: personal-project-1
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Predict Prototype Test · Theory Prototype Test
steps.instruction:
theory case — A polished coded prototype appears persuasive, but it tests no predeclared assumption and quietly becomes production code after one positive comment. The theory variation 4 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Run a protocol with success, failure, observation, accessibility accommodation, and interpretation criteria set before the session.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Prototype Test contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Prototype Test · Theory Prototype Test.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for run a protocol with success, failure, observation, accessibility accommodation, and interpretation criteria set before the session. For Predict Prototype Test · Theory Prototype Test, keep Prototype Test connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: A polished coded prototype appears persuasive, but it tests no predeclared assumption and quietly becomes production code after one positive comment. The theory variation 4 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Run a protocol with success, failure, observation, accessibility accommodation, and interpretation criteria set before the session.

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Prototype Test condition decided your prediction.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: ACM IEEE AAAI Computer Science Curricula 2023 — Controls competency-based progression, project work, professional practice, ethics, accessibility, security, testing, and the integration of knowledge with observable performance.

content.text: Run a protocol with success, failure, observation, accessibility accommodation, and interpretation criteria set before the session. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name the stakeholder decision, affected people, authority, assumption, revision, artifact, observable behavior, changed case, rejected failure, accessibility, security, privacy, operation, recovery, contribution, residual risk, and transfer limit; then calibrate the public claim to that exact evidence.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A polished coded prototype appears persuasive, but it tests no predeclared assumption and quietly becomes production code after one positive comment. The worked example variation 4 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Run a protocol with success, failure, observation, accessibility accommodation, and interpretation criteria set before the session. A defensible solution applies Prototype Test, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to ranked assumption map and prototype evidence.

content.code:
workspace: portfolio
project-id: changed-product-2
stakeholder: affected-user review panel
decision: continue only if the changed task meets its outcome and guardrails
artifact: revision-bound evidence packet
claim: one bounded behavior survives the changed context
verify: reproduce the task, inspect the artifact, and compare the predeclared measure
changed-case: different access need, input, constraint, and dependency failure
failure: reject plausible output without causal, human, recovery, or ownership evidence
repair: isolate the first unsupported transition, correct it, and add a regression
owner: named learner and stakeholder reviewer
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research and release environment
content.caption: Runnable transformation example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Any positive participant comment validates the prototype. This fails the Prototype Test contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.value: ACM IEEE AAAI Computer Science Curricula 2023 (CS2023 final report) — Controls competency-based progression, project work, professional practice, ethics, accessibility, security, testing, and the integration of knowledge with observable performance.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Run a protocol with success, failure, observation, accessibility accommodation, and interpretation criteria set before the session.

stimulus.title: Prototype Test failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: Any positive participant comment validates the prototype.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Run a protocol with success, failure, observation, accessibility accommodation, and interpretation criteria set before the session.

options.text: Reconstruct the conditions for Prototype Test, gather a counterexample, repair the artifact, and add a regression case.
Keep the artifact unchanged and rewrite the explanation so the current output appears intentional. That claim does not survive the repair the prototype test defect evidence boundary.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

steps.title: Defend the Prototype Test model · Theory Prototype Test
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Prototype Test: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A polished coded prototype appears persuasive, but it tests no predeclared assumption and quietly becomes production code after one positive comment. The theory variation 5 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Run a protocol with success, failure, observation, accessibility accommodation, and interpretation criteria set before the session.

Evidence target: Defend the Prototype Test model · Theory Prototype Test.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Defend the Prototype Test model · Theory Prototype Test, keep Prototype Test connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Run a protocol with success, failure, observation, accessibility accommodation, and interpretation criteria set before the session.
